claimText: AdFalciVax is India's first indigenous multi-stage malaria vaccine.
evidenceExcerpt: AdFalciVax is India's first indigenous multi-stage malaria vaccine.
examUse: Statement-based question on indigenous vaccines in India.

claimText: AdFalciVax targets the Pre-erythrocytic (liver) stage and the Transmission (sexual) stage of the Plasmodium falciparum parasite.
evidenceExcerpt: It targets two critical stages of Plasmodium falciparum: the Pre-erythrocytic (liver) stage and the Transmission (sexual) stage.
examUse: Statement-based question on the mechanism of action of the AdFalciVax vaccine.

claimText: A recombinant chimeric vaccine combines antigens from different stages of a parasite's life cycle into a single immunogen.
evidenceExcerpt: It is a Recombinant Chimeric Vaccine, combining antigens from different life cycle stages into a single immunogen.
examUse: Definition or concept matching question on vaccine technologies.
distractorHooks: Uses mRNA to target a single antigen

claimText: Malaria is caused by Plasmodium parasites, with five species affecting humans, and spreads through infected female Anopheles mosquitoes.
evidenceExcerpt: Malaria is caused by Plasmodium parasites and spreads through infected female Anopheles mosquitoes. Five Plasmodium species cause malaria in humans, including P. falciparum and P. vivax.
examUse: Basic science statement-based question on disease vectors and pathogens.
